message:
fix: boot starts Deacon directly when detected missing (gt-mlmys)

Previously, Boot's degraded triage only reported deacon-missing and
relied on the daemon to restart Deacon on the next heartbeat cycle
(up to 3 minutes away). The daemon's ensureDeaconRunning runs before
Boot, so if Deacon dies after that check, recovery was delayed.

Now Boot uses deacon.Manager.Start() directly when it detects Deacon
is missing, providing immediate recovery. Also updates the AI formula
(mol-boot-triage) START action to use `gt deacon start` instead of
just logging.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -148,9 +148,9 @@ If you're making progress, please update your agent bead to reflect activity."
 
 **START**
 ```bash
-# Deacon is dead - daemon will restart it
-# Just log that we detected this
-echo "Boot detected dead Deacon session - daemon will restart"
+# Deacon is dead - start it immediately
+gt deacon start
+echo "Boot started Deacon session"
 ```
 
 Record action taken for status update.
